sentences.text: Hey Todd.
Hey, how are you doing today?
Good, how are you?
Doing great.
We messed something up again with the renewal, it sounds like.
Yeah, I think there's there's just a little disconnect there.
All right, we'll make sure it doesn't happen again next year, but, um, I, I figured it would make sense to get together because we have a lot that's, that's new.
Yeah, give you a product update and then we can uh take care of those terms and get, get whatever sorted out that we need to.
Perfect.
Sounds good.
Um, awesome.
I know we had met, I think in January we had talked about a few things at that point which were uh potential data feed over to you all.
Yep.
And then, um, a new like module within the tool uh that has like research functions.
Yep, I just wanted to update on both of those because um Q4 of last year was kind of our launching point for this new uh tool.
Uh, that we're calling it explore right now, but will likely be named to something like Invest.

And uh so are are you guys remind me, are you on, uh, are you on cos or do you use like a market analytics, um, what are you using in terms of like broader market uh discovery or or sourcing projects?
Um, I, I, you know, I think they use a multiple.
I think they use like Yardi Matrix.
I know that's kind of out there and I think Costar as well.
So there's I think there's a few different products that are used by, you know, several people, but myself, um, is basically just your the market survey component, but.
I have access to the other ones.
I just, I don't really, I use market or Y matrix a little bit, but um like the brokers and all that other stuff, we, you kind of have that floating around the office and and other departments.
Yeah, gotcha.
OK.

So just, just like for your knowledge direction we're headed is right, you've got this market survey section of platform, uh, you have this research section of platform which is for non-owned managed, so you get to the point where you're like ready to underwrite or see how uh uh.

Um, you know, what, what a piece of like if you're on a land investment team, you wanna see how a submarket would perform with certain build factors you're doing, or if you're looking at an acquisition or if you're, you know, one of our PMCs, you're looking at change of management targets.
Um, this essentially is where you can look up any property in the database and create the same set of reports that you're getting, uh, in the market survey section right now.
Right.

And then a third component of this was introduced, and I don't know if we, we covered this, but Um, this eventually will trend towards, uh, competing with a co-star or market analytics or or Yardi Matrix.
So, um, right now it's relatively rudimentary, but you'll see product introduced to this, you know, uh, every couple of weeks here for the next few quarters.
Um, yeah, and essentially what you have is the ability to like, you know, look at a, a full, full market, um, rent to performance, supply information, demographic information.
Uh, eventually directly out of here, you'll have a, a very co-star feel to it.

So every single piece of inventory within here will have a, um, you know, a set of details behind it, which will include anything from like transactions data to ownership data to taxes to expenses, that's all being worked out right now.
OK.
But I wanted to get this out in front of you because as we're looking at arrangements,
essentially how this will work is like this my properties section will be split off with its own cost, uh, and these two sections will have a cost associated with them, uh, as well.
OK, yeah, I think from our last conversation we had to meet like a minimum spend.
There was some thresholds too, which obviously we're not at currently, so
yeah, is that still in play?
Yeah, I could show you like a working version of kind of kind of how we're thinking about this.
I don't know if you see that, but so yeah so like that market survey is gonna be standalone, right?

Uh, we're gonna eventually kind of move to this minimum, and they're getting a certain number of users associated with it, and then you have this invest, which is like bundled here, and then if you want both product lines, right, there's that associated cost to it.
OK.
So, I guess like remind me how many people are at Franklin are are actually like in the tool on a daily basis.
Um, I mean, right now it's mainly focused on our multi-family, you know, components, so there's several people, you know, the property staff get They get a, you know, report, they can log in daily.
Do they log in daily?
Not sure, but they have the ability to do that.
I know my regional logs in daily, they kind of look at that.
I, I look at it a lot.
So, you know, we have, you know, a handful of people say, uh, right now,

um, you know, our multi-family portfolios has shrunk down a little bit, but you know, it should be growing here in the near future, so that's why we're kind of You know, that's why you and I talked previously and that's why I'm still kind of keeping my thumb here on the pulse to kind of see, see what's available and, you know, when we kind of get more properties, you know, to go I want to make sure we have the tools in place.
Yeah.
So that's kind of was thinking too, because like we can just we can recontract it per unit like we've been doing.

Um, the other option is just to kind of like flat rate that out on the annual cost and then you're getting all those product lines in it, um, and The reason I want to meet is like if we do it earlier, it's probably gonna be more advantageous for when we break those two products out, and each of them have a cost associated with them, if that makes sense.
Yeah, I think, I think currently we're at like $1 a unit or something like that.
So what you were showing was, was basically that price for everything.
Yeah, exactly.

So that's like if we could get that, if we get you on, um, you know, like the, the combined package of that, it's probably be most advantageous, and if you did it on a longer term, it's gonna be even more advantageous, but like I can summarize all this for you so you have it
on it, but.

This tool right now, right?
Like it looks, it's it's fairly simple.
You're getting like a snapshot, some historicals.
Right, you've got supply information, you've got demographic information.

Um, where this is headed with the financials and the ownership, contact information and uh rent forecasting and uh data and visualizations around, you know, market trends, um, it's gonna be a much more comprehensive tool that kind of commands its own cost.
So, depending on if that's valuable to you all, if, if that would be used, um, the idea is that you can, can get out ahead of it if you'd like.
And then does that, does, does that include the data feed as well, like, so if we wanted to bump up to it and and and pull it into our.

Yeah, it doesn't, but we could, I mean like we could consolidate, we could just find a way to like consolidate cost to like where it makes sense for you, um, OK, because that's the thing that commands that 1000 minimum, so maybe we do like, you know, you had a couple 100 bucks on that and get the data feed included.
So I can just like run some numbers and get you some packages.
Um, for now we need to get your one site back up, so I'll just get you the paperwork on that.
That can get done, you know.
And so, so the thing is like that site is active, right?
Like we,
we, we get.

So kind of, kind of to go backwards, right?
So, property staff manager reached out to me and said, hey, I haven't been getting invoices for for the Morrison, um, you know, do you have a way to get them?
So I've been kind of going back and forth.

So we've kind of gone back and forth for a while on that and then I know previous to that, the property manager reached out to y'all and they're like, yeah, you don't have any services with us, and they're like, we don't have any services.

So it's just kind of going around, around, around, around, and then, you know, uh, they finally looped you in, which I guess I should have went to you first and I, but I just thought that, you know, go to the people that that send out the invoices and, you know, sort of thing, so.
Um,

but then, you know, I, I think there was just a little confusion on, you know, it's this isn't just for the Morrison, right, that we have all their properties that we're getting billed on, so I just want to make sure that there's no disconnect with the other ones and they don't get, you know, kicked off either.

When did that transition, October, I think it was something like that, the end of October.

no, that was December, maybe like the end of December, I think is when it transitioned away.
OK, so sir, OK, so I'm not seeing that platform.

Correct, yeah.
OK, yeah.

Yeah, I think everything else we have is just the Morrison, so OK.

But you know, take a look at that, you know, let's just make sure that we don't get disconnected again, you know, like, I mean, even though we're not disconnected, we're still getting the products, so um.
Yeah.

I'll summarize that for you.
I'll summarize just kind of like the direction things are headed and what costs associated with that would be depending on if you want to move that direction with us.
Uh, and other than that, I'm, I'm all good.
Any anything.

So and
um once you send over the info, you know, I'll take a look at the the products and stuff like that and talk it over with,
you know, the rest of the team and we'll go from there.
